pattern_recognition: This problem falls under the category of dynamic programming and string manipulation. The key signals include the need to identify symmetrical patterns in substrings and the requirement to optimize the search for the longest palindrome.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check every possible substring and determine if it is a palindrome, keeping track of the longest one found.
approaches.algorithm: Generate all substrings, check each for palindrome property, and compare lengths.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Expand around potential centers of palindromes to find the longest one efficiently without generating all substrings.
approaches.algorithm: For each character (and each pair of characters), expand outwards to check for palindromes.

dry_run: For the input 'babad', start with the first character 'b', check substrings 'b', 'ba', 'bab', 'baba', 'babad'. 'bab' is a palindrome. Move to 'a', check 'a', 'ab', 'aba', 'abad', 'abada'. 'aba' is a palindrome. Continue with 'd'. The longest found is 'bab' or 'aba'.
edge_cases: An empty string should return an empty result. A single character should return that character. Strings with all identical characters should return the entire string. Large inputs should be handled without performance issues.
common_pitfalls: 1) Failing to check for even-length palindromes. 2) Not accounting for single character strings. 3) Inefficient substring generation leading to time complexity issues.
